At one time the commission had
600 men and over 100 teams at
work.


Then rolls OF the Indians had to
be made. Neither the government
nor the tribes had authentic rolls.
The tribal rolls had been brazenly
padded with straw names in order
to draw Indian money. Tams took
out the dummies.


Many Indians would take to the
brush as soon as a government
party came into the neighborhood.
Ten years of persistent effort has

Added to this difficulty in get
ting the Indians to enroll was the
persistent efforts OF other claimants
who were not entitled to get on
the rolls so they could get lands.
There were 100000 applications for
citizenship, and out of that number
100000 were finally admitted.
